_comment: Functional benefic/malefic (Karyesha) classification rules, consumed by functionalNatureRuleEvaluator.js. Rather than hardcoding a static table of results per each of the 12 possible Lagnas, this is derived algorithmically from house lordship relative to the Lagna, reusing the existing kendra/trikona/dusthana house groups from rules/houses.json — keeping this scalable and config-driven per the Priority 3 requirement ('without hardcoding logic').

priorityRules.description: Lord of the 1st house (Lagna) is always functionally benefic, regardless of any other house it also owns.
Owns both a kendra house (1/4/7/10) and a trikona house (1/5/9) - the strongest functional benefic role (e.g. Mars for Cancer/Leo Lagna, Saturn for Libra/Taurus Lagna).
Owns a trikona house (5th/9th) and no dusthana house (6th/8th/12th).
Owns only dusthana house(s) (6th/8th/12th), with no kendra/trikona lordship.

Owns only kendra house(s) (4th/7th/10th) with no trikona/dusthana lordship - removes the planet's natural malefic tendency (Kendradhipati Dosha context) without granting strong extra benefic strength.
Owns both a trikona and a dusthana house - mixed classical results; treated as neutral in this foundation phase rather than asserting a specific tie-break.
Fallback when no other rule applies.

nodesFallback.description: Rahu/Ketu own no sign classically, so house-lordship rules do not apply to them. Foundation approximation: derive their functional nature from their natural (Naisargika) dignity, softened to neutral when placed in a kendra/trikona house from the Lagna and kept/worsened to malefic when placed in a dusthana house.
